content_en:
*Urawah is reported to have said: "Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, may Allah be pleased with him, freed seven people who were subjected to torture in Allah's Cause. It was about him that the verse was revealed:

(VY: hl Bu ives Bayh "But those most devoted to Allah shall be-removed far from it (i. e. the Hell-Fire)" up to the end of the Surah. (Reported by Ibn Abi Haatim and At-Tabarani.) * Abdullaah Ibn Az-Zubayr states: The Qur'anic verse, (VA: dw "Sid ER oe oles 9 Gy" "And have in their minds no favor from anyone for which a

reward is expected in return "(Al-Layl: 19) up to the end of the Surah, was revealed in reference to Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, may

Allah be pleased with him. (Reported by Al-Bazzaar.)

~Aa'ishah; may Allah be pleased with her, is reported to have said that Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, never broke his oath until Allah, Exalted be He, revealed the expiation for breaking one's oath. (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

Ibn ~ Abbaas notes that the verse, (VOA:O) se JT) “pu re) goby sts"

"...and consult with them upon the conduct of affairs" (Al--Imraan: 158) refers to Abu Bakr and *Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both. (Reported by Al-Haakim.)

On the authority of Ibn Shawbadh who said that the verse, (EN: gF By "Oe Sh alls GE Lay"

"But for him win feareth the standing before his Lord there are two gardens " (Ar-Rahmaan: 46) was reveled concerning Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him. (Reported by Ibn Abi Haatim.)

Usayd Ibn Safwaan reports that ~ Ali (Ibn Abi Taalib) said concerning the verse,

(PY: phy "Osa (BOS wy Ghee GiLaly oe ally”

"And he who brings that truth and he who confirms (and supports) it such are the men who do right." (Az-Zumar: 33) “he who brings the Truth" refers to Prophet Muhammad, peace and blessings be upon him, and "he who confirms (and

supports)" it refers to Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, may Allah be pleased with him. (Reported by Al-Bazzaar and Ibn ~ Asaakir.) Ibn ~Umar and Ibn *Abbaas are reported to have said

(feral) "el ite” "...and the righteous among the believers" (AT-Tahreem: 4)

was revealed concerning Abu Bakr and “Umar (Ibn AlKhattaab)

In his exegesis of the Quran, ~Abdullaah Ibn Abi Humayd reports Mujahid to have said; "When the verse,

Md ts yal a ale igo UT yall Bl wot le O had anu ah Oy (Choire D)

"Allah and his Angels send blessing on the Prophet: O ye that believe send ye blessings on him, and salute him with all respect" (Al-Ahzaab: 56) was revealed, Abu Bakr said, Never was anything good sent down you, Then the verse was revealed:

Ce Sy Bh hh allt of Ke Bed Kirin Sle Shad coal 9h" (EMP "sy

"He it is Who send blessings on you, as-do His angels, that He may bring you out from the depths' of Darkness into Light: and He is Full of Mercy to the Believers. " (Al-Ahzaab: 43)

“Ali Ibn Al-Husayn is reported to have said that the

(EV: gab) “Sle pe ge UB YB paysite BU EI"

"And we shall remove from their hearts any lurking sense of injury: (they will be) brothers (joyfully) facing each other on thrones (of dignity)" (Al-Hijr: 47) was revealed concerning Abu Bakr, ~Umar, and ~ Ali. (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

On the authority of Ibn ~ Abbaas: The verses, (V0: H YUL dy] Sully OLIy oy

"We have enjoined on man kindness to his parents" up to

(Ve "Ogle SGT ced Glatt a4"

"...@ promise of truth, which was made to them (in this life)." (Al-Ahqaaf: 16) were revealed in reference to Abu Bakr AsSideeq, may Allah be pleased with him. (Reported by Ibn * Asaakir.)

Ibn * Uyaynah is reported to have said: Allah reproached all the companions who failed to defend the Prophet, peace and

blessings be upon him, except Abu Bakr. Then he recited the verse,

"If ye help not (your Leader), (it is no matter): for Allah did indeed help him, when the Unbelievers drove him out: he had

no more than one companion: they tow were in the Cave, and he said to his companion, have no fear, for Allah is with us: then Allah sent down his peace upon him." (At-Tawbah: 40)

Hadiths on the Virtues of Abu Bakr Alone:

Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him. is reported to have said, "I heard the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, saying: "Whoever spends a pair of something in Allah's Cause will be called from all the gates of Paradise," O Allah's slave! This is good.' He who is amongst those who pray, will be called from the gate of prayer (in Paradise); he who is from the people who perform Jihad (in the Cause of Allah) will be called from the gate of Jihad; he who is from those who give in charity will be called from the gate of charity; and he who is amongst those who observe fast will be called from the gate of Ar-Rayyaan." Abu Bakr said: "He who will called from all these gates is in no need of anything: will anyone be called from all those gates, O Messenger of Allah?" "Yes," replied the Prophet, "and I hope you will be among them, Abu Bakr."

Abu Sa*eed Al-Khudryy, may Allah be pleased with him, reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "The most bountiful of men unto me in his companionship and sacrifice of his wealth is Abu Bakr. If I were to take an intimate friend other than my Lord, I would take Abu Bakr. But what binds us is the brotherhood of Islam and its love." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.)

Abu Ad-Dardaa', may Allah be pleased with him, narrates: I was sitting with the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, when Abu Bakr came, greeted us, and then said: "There was a dispute between me and * Umar, and I made him angry. Then I felt remorse, followed him, and asked him to

Ibn “Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, reports the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, to have said: "On the Day of Resurrection, Allah will not look at a person who drags his clothes of ostentation." On that Abu Bakr said, "O Messenger of Allah! One side of my clothes hangs loose if I do not hold them place. The Prophet said, ‘You are not one of those who do that for the sake for ostentation. "(Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, narrates: Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, once said: "Who amongst you is fasting to day?" Abu Bakr said, that The Prophet asked again, "Who amongst you has been in a funeral procession?" Abu Bakr said, that The Prophet asked further, "Who amongst you has served food to the needy?” Abu Bakr said, that The Prophet asked again, ‘Who amongst you has visited a sick person today?" Abu Bakr said that he did all of

these acts. Thereupon Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "Whoever combines in himself (all these virtues) will enter Paradise." (Reported by Moslem.)

Ibn “Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, quotes the Prophet, peace and blessing be upon him, to have said to Abu Bakr: "You are my companion at the Al-Hawd (Fount)! and my companion in the cave." (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi)

*Abdullaah Ibn Ahmad, may Allah be pleased with him, reports with a good chain of narrators, that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said, "Abu Bakr is my intimate friend and my affable companion in the cave."

Abu Hurayrah, may Allah be pleased with him, reports the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, to have said: "What an excellent man Abu Bakr is, what an excellent man ‘Umar is... "(Reported by Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawood and AtTirmidhi.)

Abu Sa*eed Al-Khudryy reports Allah's Messenger to have said: "People of higher ranks (in Paradise) will be seen by those beneath them the way you can detect a bright star shining in the sky. Abu Bakr and “Umar will be among them. How excellent they are!" (Reported by Ahmad, Al-Tirmidhi, and Ibn Hibbaan.)

Anas Tbn Malik and * Ali Ibn Abi Taalib, may Allah be pleased with them both, narrate that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said to Abu Bakr and *Umar:

‘This is the Fount (of abundance) which Allah mentions in Surah, "Al-Kawthar",

On Judgment Day, rightcous Muslims will be allowed to drink from this Fount, after which they will not grow thirsty ever again.

"Those two will be the chiefs of the old men in Paradise, both

from old and modern times, excluding Messengers and Prophets... Do not tell them of that, “Ali.” (Reported by AtTirmidhi.)

Anas Ibn Malik narrates, " The Prophet ascended the mountain of Uhud accompanied by Abu Bakr, ~Umar and *Uthmaan. The mountain shook beneath them. The Prophet hit it with his foot and said, "O Uhud! Be firm, for on you there is none but a Prophet, a Siddeeq (Abu Bakr) and a martyr (i.e. and two martyrs)." (Reported by Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawood and Al-Tirmidhi.)

Ibn ~Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both, reports that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "The most merciful of my nation to my nation is Abu Bakr; the firmest in abiding by Allah's Commands is “Umar; the most bashful is ~Uthmaan; and the best in judgment is “Ali.” (Reported by Ahmad, Al-Tirmidhi and An-Nasaa’i.)

Sa*eed Ibn Jubayr, may Allah be pleased with him, narrates: "I recited before the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, the verse,
content_id:
Diriwayatkan bahwa 'Urwah berkata: "Abu Bakr As-Siddiq radhiyallahu 'anhu membebaskan tujuh orang yang disiksa karena membela agama Allah. Tentang beliau, ayat ini diturunkan:

(QS. Al-Lail: 17) "Dan kelak akan dijauhkan orang yang paling bertakwa dari neraka itu" hingga akhir surah. (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Abi Hatim dan At-Tabarani.) Abdullah bin Az-Zubair menyatakan: Ayat Al-Quran, (QS. Al-Lail: 19) "Padahal tidak ada seorang pun memberikan suatu nikmat kepadanya yang harus dibalasnya"

hingga akhir surah, diturunkan berkenaan dengan Abu Bakr As-Siddiq,

radhiyallahu 'anhu. (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzar.)

'Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha diriwayatkan berkata bahwa Abu Bakr radhiyallahu 'anhu tidak pernah melanggar sumpahnya sampai Allah Subhanahu wa Ta'ala menurunkan ayat tentang kafarat (tebusan) bagi pelanggaran sumpah. (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Ibnu Abbas mencatat bahwa ayat, (QS. Ali 'Imran: 159)

"...dan bermusyawarahlah dengan mereka dalam urusan itu" merujuk kepada Abu Bakr dan 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhuma. (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Hakim.)

Berdasarkan riwayat dari Ibnu Syawbadz yang berkata bahwa ayat, (QS. Ar-Rahman: 46)

"Dan bagi orang yang takut akan saat menghadap Tuhannya ada dua surga" diturunkan berkenaan dengan Abu Bakr radhiyallahu 'anhu. (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Abi Hatim.)

Usaid bin Safwan meriwayatkan bahwa 'Ali (bin Abi Thalib) berkata mengenai ayat,

(QS. Az-Zumar: 33)

"Dan orang yang membawa kebenaran dan orang yang membenarkannya, mereka itulah orang-orang yang bertakwa." "Orang yang membawa kebenaran" merujuk kepada Nabi Muhammad shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan "orang yang membenarkan

dan mendukungnya" merujuk kepada Abu Bakr As-Siddiq radhiyallahu 'anhu. (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bazzar dan Ibnu 'Asakir.) Ibnu 'Umar dan Ibnu Abbas diriwayatkan telah berkata bahwa

(QS. At-Tahrim: 4) "...dan orang-orang salih di antara orang-orang mukmin"

diturunkan berkenaan dengan Abu Bakr dan 'Umar (bin Al-Khaththab).

Dalam tafsir Al-Qurannya, Abdullah bin Abi Humaid meriwayatkan dari Mujahid yang berkata: "Ketika ayat,

(QS. Al-Ahzab: 56)

"Sesungguhnya Allah dan para malaikat-Nya bershalawat untuk Nabi. Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, bershalawatlah kamu untuk Nabi dan ucapkanlah salam penghormatan kepadanya" diturunkan, Abu Bakr berkata, 'Tidak pernah sesuatu yang baik diturunkan kepadamu.' Kemudian turunlah ayat:

(QS. Al-Ahzab: 43)

"Dialah yang memberi rahmat kepadamu dan para malaikat-Nya (memohonkan ampunan untukmu), supaya Dia mengeluarkan kamu dari kegelapan kepada cahaya (yang terang). Dan Dia Maha Penyayang kepada orang-orang yang beriman."

'Ali bin Al-Husain diriwayatkan telah berkata bahwa

(QS. Al-Hijr: 47)

"Dan Kami cabut segala macam dendam yang berada di dalam dada mereka; mereka bersaudara duduk berhadap-hadapan di atas dipan-dipan" diturunkan berkenaan dengan Abu Bakr, 'Umar, dan 'Ali. (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Berdasarkan riwayat dari Ibnu Abbas: Ayat-ayat, (QS. Al-Ahqaf: 15)

"Kami perintahkan kepada manusia supaya berbuat baik kepada kedua orang tuanya" hingga

(QS. Al-Ahqaf: 16)

"...janji yang benar yang telah dijanjikan kepada mereka (di dunia ini)." diturunkan berkenaan dengan Abu Bakr As-Siddiq radhiyallahu 'anhu. (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Asakir.)

Ibnu 'Uyainah diriwayatkan telah berkata: Allah mencela semua sahabat yang gagal membela Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam

kecuali Abu Bakr. Kemudian beliau membaca ayat,

"Jikalau kamu tidak menolongnya (Muhammad), maka sesungguhnya Allah telah menolongnya (yaitu) ketika orang-orang kafir mengusirnya (dari Makkah), sedang dia salah seorang dari dua orang ketika keduanya berada dalam gua, di waktu dia berkata kepada temannya: 'Janganlah berduka cita, sesungguhnya Allah bersama kita.' Maka Allah menurunkan ketenangan-Nya kepadanya." (QS. At-Taubah: 40)

Hadits-hadits tentang Keutamaan Abu Bakr Secara Khusus:

Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu diriwayatkan berkata, "Aku mendengar Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: 'Barang siapa yang menginfakkan sepasang sesuatu di jalan Allah, dia akan dipanggil dari semua pintu surga,' 'Wahai hamba Allah! Ini adalah kebaikan.' Siapa yang termasuk orang-orang yang shalat, akan dipanggil dari pintu shalat (di surga); siapa yang termasuk orang-orang yang berjihad (di jalan Allah) akan dipanggil dari pintu jihad; siapa yang termasuk orang-orang yang bersedekah akan dipanggil dari pintu sedekah; dan siapa yang termasuk orang-orang yang berpuasa akan dipanggil dari pintu Ar-Rayyan." Abu Bakr berkata: "Orang yang dipanggil dari semua pintu itu tidak membutuhkan apa-apa lagi: apakah ada seseorang yang akan dipanggil dari semua pintu itu, wahai Rasulullah?" "Ya," jawab Nabi, "dan aku berharap engkau termasuk di antara mereka, wahai Abu Bakr."

Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: "Orang yang paling dermawan kepadaku dalam persahabatan dan pengorbanan hartanya adalah Abu Bakr. Seandainya aku boleh mengambil seorang khalil (sahabat karib) selain Tuhanku, niscaya aku akan mengambil Abu Bakr. Namun yang mengikat kami adalah persaudaraan Islam dan kecintaannya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Abu Ad-Darda' radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan: Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam ketika Abu Bakr datang, memberi salam kepada kami, lalu berkata: "Ada perselisihan antara aku dan 'Umar, dan aku membuatnya marah. Kemudian aku merasa menyesal, mengikutinya, dan memintanya untuk

Ibnu 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: "Pada Hari Kiamat, Allah tidak akan memandang orang yang menyeret pakaiannya karena sombong." Abu Bakr berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Salah satu sisi pakaianku kendur jika aku tidak menahannya." Nabi bersabda, 'Engkau bukan termasuk orang yang melakukannya karena sombong.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan: Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam suatu hari bersabda: "Siapa di antara kalian yang berpuasa hari ini?" Abu Bakr menjawab bahwa dia berpuasa. Nabi bertanya lagi, "Siapa di antara kalian yang mengikuti prosesi jenazah?" Abu Bakr menjawab bahwa dia mengikutinya. Nabi bertanya lagi, "Siapa di antara kalian yang memberi makan orang miskin?" Abu Bakr menjawab bahwa dia melakukannya. Nabi bertanya lagi, "Siapa di antara kalian yang menjenguk orang sakit hari ini?" Abu Bakr menjawab bahwa dia melakukan semua

itu. Maka Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: "Barang siapa yang menggabungkan semua (kebajikan ini) dalam dirinya, niscaya dia akan masuk surga." (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.)

Ibnu 'Umar radhiyallahu 'anhu mengutip Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda kepada Abu Bakr: "Engkau adalah sahabatku di Al-Haudh (Telaga) dan sahabatku di dalam gua." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi)

Abdullah bin Ahmad radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan dengan sanad yang baik, bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Abu Bakr adalah khalil (sahabat karib)ku dan teman baikku di dalam gua."

Abu Hurairah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: "Sebaik-baik manusia adalah Abu Bakr, sebaik-baik manusia adalah 'Umar..." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawud dan At-Tirmidzi.)

Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda: "Orang-orang yang berkedudukan tinggi (di surga) akan terlihat oleh orang-orang di bawah mereka sebagaimana kalian melihat bintang terang bersinar di langit. Abu Bakr dan 'Umar akan berada di antara mereka. Alangkah baiknya mereka berdua!" (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, At-Tirmidzi, dan Ibnu Hibban.)

Anas bin Malik dan 'Ali bin Abi Thalib radhiyallahu 'anhuma meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda kepada Abu Bakr dan 'Umar:

"Ini adalah Telaga (kelimpahan) yang Allah sebutkan dalam Surah Al-Kautsar,

Pada Hari Kiamat, kaum muslimin yang salih akan diizinkan minum dari Telaga ini, setelah itu mereka tidak akan pernah haus lagi.

"Mereka berdua akan menjadi pemimpin orang-orang tua di surga, baik dari zaman dahulu maupun zaman sekarang, kecuali para Rasul dan para Nabi... Jangan beritahukan hal itu kepada mereka, wahai 'Ali." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi.)

Anas bin Malik meriwayatkan, "Nabi mendaki Gunung Uhud ditemani Abu Bakr, 'Umar, dan 'Utsman. Gunung itu bergetar di bawah mereka. Nabi menghentakkan kakinya dan bersabda, 'Tenanglah wahai Uhud! Karena di atasmu tidak ada kecuali seorang Nabi, seorang Siddiq (Abu Bakr) dan seorang syahid (yakni dua orang syahid).'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari, Abu Dawud dan At-Tirmidzi.)

Ibnu Umar, semoga Allah meridhai keduanya, meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Yang paling penyayang di antara umatku terhadap umatku adalah Abu Bakar; yang paling tegas dalam menaati perintah Allah adalah Umar; yang paling pemalu adalah Utsman; dan yang paling baik dalam memberikan keputusan adalah Ali." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, At-Tirmidzi dan An-Nasa'i.)

Sa'id bin Jubair, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan: "Aku membaca di hadapan Nabi ﷺ ayat,
